description: >
  Verifies the 128 KB SRAM subsystem (4 × CF_SRAM_1024x32 banks) of
  mspm33c321a_derivative. Covers base-address read/write, full address
  walking over the 128 KB range, sequential and random data-integrity
  patterns, and concurrent CPU + DMA access without corruption.
checklist_ids:
  - MEM-01
  - MEM-02
  - MEM-03
  - MEM-04
test_names:
  - test_mem_base_rw
  - test_mem_addr_walk
  - test_mem_data_integrity
  - test_mem_cpu_dma_concurrent
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices: []

sram_params:
  base_addr: 0x20000000
  size_bytes: 0x00020000   # 128 KB
  bank_count: 4
  bank_size_bytes: 0x00008000   # 32 KB each

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset and allow bus fabric to initialise
  - step: 2
    action: ahb_write_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    data: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Write known word at SRAM base address
  - step: 3
    action: ahb_read_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    expected: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Read back base address and verify data
  - step: 4
    action: ahb_address_walk
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    end_addr: 0x2001FFFF
    pattern: ADDR_AS_DATA
    description: Write address-as-data to every word; read back and compare
  - step: 5
    action: ahb_burst_write
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Sequential burst write with pseudo-random data (LFSR seed 0xCAFE)
  - step: 6
    action: ahb_burst_read_check
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Burst read and compare against expected LFSR sequence
  - step: 7
    action: concurrent_dma_cpu_access
    dma_channel: 0
    dma_src: 0x20000000
    dma_dst: 0x20010000
    dma_len_bytes: 0x8000
    cpu_rw_addr: 0x20018000
    description: CPU reads/writes upper SRAM bank while DMA copies lower bank

checks:
  - id: CHK-MEM-01
    checklist_id: MEM-01
    description: Read-back of 0xDEADBEEF at base address 0x20000000 matches write
    expected: data == 0xDEADBEEF
  - id: CHK-MEM-02
    checklist_id: MEM-02
    description: All 32768 word addresses in 128 KB read back their written value
    expected: Zero mismatches in full address walk
  - id: CHK-MEM-03
    checklist_id: MEM-03
    description: All 32768 LFSR-pattern words compare correctly after burst write
    expected: Zero byte mismatches
  - id: CHK-MEM-04
    checklist_id: MEM-04
    description: DMA copy completes correctly and CPU data is uncorrupted
    expected: DMA destination matches source; CPU reads are consistent

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-MEM checks PASS
  - No AHB HRESP=ERROR during SRAM access in valid range
  - DMA transfer-complete flag set within expected cycle count
"}

function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_gpio_basic.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_gpio_basic
description: >
  Verifies basic GPIO functionality across all three ports (GPIOA, GPIOB, GPIOC)
  of mspm33c321a_derivative. Covers output direction control and toggling on each
  of 48 pins, input read-back driven by an external gpio_stimulus model, and
  power-on reset default state (all pins Hi-Z input).
checklist_ids:
  - GPIO-01
  - GPIO-02
  - GPIO-03
  - GPIO-04
  - GPIO-05
  - GPIO-06
  - GPIO-07
test_names:
  - test_gpio_porta_output
  - test_gpio_porta_input
  - test_gpio_portb_output
  - test_gpio_portb_input
  - test_gpio_portc_output
  - test_gpio_portc_input
  - test_gpio_reset_state
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: gpioa_in[15:0]
    params:
      drive_pattern: WALKING_ONES
      hold_cycles: 4
  - name: jtag_probe_model_b
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: gpiob_in[15:0]
    params:
      drive_pattern: WALKING_ONES
      hold_cycles: 4
  - name: jtag_probe_model_c
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: gpioc_in[15:0]
    params:
      drive_pattern: WALKING_ONES
      hold_cycles: 4

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: check_reset_state
    description: Immediately after reset assert, sample OE registers for all three ports
    expected_oe: 0x0000   # all inputs (Hi-Z)
  - step: 2
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; GPIO ports remain in input mode
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOA
    register: DIRCFG
    value: 0xFFFF
    description: Configure all GPIOA pins as output
  - step: 4
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOA
    register: DOUT
    value: 0xAAAA
    description: Write alternating pattern to GPIOA output register
  - step: 5
    action: sample_pad_output
    port: gpioa_out
    expected: 0xAAAA
    description: Observe GPIOA pad output signals match written pattern
  - step: 6
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    pattern: WALKING_ONES
    description: Drive GPIOA pads with walking-ones pattern via external model
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: GPIOA
    register: DIN
    description: Read GPIOA input register; compare against driven pattern
  - step: 8
    action: repeat_steps
    steps: [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
    ports: [GPIOB, GPIOC]
    devices: [jtag_probe_model_b, jtag_probe_model_c]
    description: Repeat output/input tests for GPIOB and GPIOC

checks:
  - id: CHK-GPIO-01
    checklist_id: GPIO-01
    description: GPIOA output pads reflect 0xAAAA written to DOUT
    expected: gpioa_out == 0xAAAA when DIRCFG=0xFFFF
  - id: CHK-GPIO-02
    checklist_id: GPIO-02
    description: GPIOA DIN reads back walking-ones pattern driven by stimulus model
    expected: DIN matches stimulus pattern on each cycle
  - id: CHK-GPIO-03
    checklist_id: GPIO-03
    description: GPIOB output pads reflect pattern written to DOUT
    expected: gpiob_out matches written value when all pins configured as output
  - id: CHK-GPIO-04
    checklist_id: GPIO-04
    description: GPIOB DIN reads back walking-ones pattern
    expected: DIN matches stimulus
  - id: CHK-GPIO-05
    checklist_id: GPIO-05
    description: GPIOC output pads reflect pattern written to DOUT
    expected: gpioc_out matches written value
  - id: CHK-GPIO-06
    checklist_id: GPIO-06
    description: GPIOC DIN reads back walking-ones pattern
    expected: DIN matches stimulus
  - id: CHK-GPIO-07
    checklist_id: GPIO-07
    description: All three port DIRCFG registers read 0x0000 immediately after reset
    expected: Default output-enable is 0 (input mode) for all 48 pins

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-GPIO checks PASS
  - No X-states on output pads in output mode
  - No APB protocol errors during CSR access
"}

function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_gpio_af_irq.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_gpio_af_irq
description: >
  Verifies GPIO alternate-function (pinmux) selection and GPIO edge-triggered
  interrupt generation on mspm33c321a_derivative. Also covers the IRQ-05 case
  for GPIOA/B/C edge triggers routing to IRQ0/1/2 in the Hazard3 PLIC.
  UART and I2C alternate functions on GPIOA and GPIOB are validated by
  confirming AF output connectivity when the AF matrix is configured.
checklist_ids:
  - GPIO-08
  - GPIO-09
  - IRQ-05
test_names:
  - test_gpio_af_pinmux
  - test_gpio_edge_irq
  - test_irq_gpio_edge
priority: P1
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: uart_model
    type: uart
    connect:
      rx_from_soc: uart0_tx_pad
      tx_to_soc: uart0_rx_pad
    params:
      baud_rate: 115200
  - name: i2c_slave_model
    type: i2c_memory
    connect:
      sda: i2c0_sda_pad
      scl: i2c0_scl_pad
    params:
      address_7bit: 0x50
      mem_size_bytes: 256
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: gpioa_in[3]
    params:
      pulse_width_ns: 200
      assert_polarity: high

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; all GPIO pins default to GPIO mode
  - step: 2
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOA_PINMUX
    register: AF_SEL_PIN0
    value: 0x1
    description: Select UART0 TX alternate function on GPIOA[0]
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOA_PINMUX
    register: AF_SEL_PIN1
    value: 0x1
    description: Select UART0 RX alternate function on GPIOA[1]
  - step: 4
    action: send_uart_byte
    device: uart_model
    data: 0xA5
    description: Send byte via UART model; verify it arrives at UART0 RX FIFO through GPIOA AF
  - step: 5
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOB_PINMUX
    register: AF_SEL_PIN4
    value: 0x2
    description: Select I2C0 SCL alternate function on GPIOB[4]
  - step: 6
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOB_PINMUX
    register: AF_SEL_PIN5
    value: 0x2
    description: Select I2C0 SDA alternate function on GPIOB[5]
  - step: 7
    action: i2c_write_byte
    device: i2c_slave_model
    addr: 0x50
    reg: 0x00
    data: 0x42
    description: Perform I2C write through GPIOB AF; verify SDA/SCL routed correctly
  - step: 8
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: GPIOA
    register: INT_CFG_PIN3
    value: 0x3
    description: Configure GPIOA[3] for both-edge interrupt
  - step: 9
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    description: Pulse GPIOA[3] high then low via stimulus model
  - step: 10
    action: check_irq_assertion
    irq_vector: 0
    description: Verify PLIC IRQ0 (GPIOA) asserted following edge trigger

checks:
  - id: CHK-GPIO-08
    checklist_id: GPIO-08
    description: UART0 byte received at UART0_RXD FIFO when GPIOA[0:1] configured for UART AF
    expected: UART0 RX FIFO contains 0xA5
  - id: CHK-GPIO-08B
    checklist_id: GPIO-08
    description: I2C0 write completes through GPIOB[4:5] AF routing
    expected: I2C slave ACKs write; data stored at slave address 0x00
  - id: CHK-GPIO-09
    checklist_id: GPIO-09
    description: Edge on GPIOA[3] sets interrupt pending flag and ISR fires
    expected: GPIOA interrupt flag set; ISR executes and clears flag
  - id: CHK-IRQ-05
    checklist_id: IRQ-05
    description: PLIC IRQ0 (GPIOA) is claimed by CPU after edge event
    expected: PLIC claim register returns IRQ0 source ID

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-GPIO-08, CHK-GPIO-09, CHK-IRQ-05 checks PASS
  - No protocol errors on UART or I2C during AF tests
  - IRQ flag clears after ISR completion
"}

function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_uart_advanced.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_uart_advanced
description: >
  Verifies advanced UART0 features on mspm33c321a_derivative: 8-deep TX FIFO
  fill and TX-empty interrupt, framing error detection, multi-baud-rate
  configuration (9600 / 115200 / 921600), and register smoke test covering
  all four UART instances (UART0–UART3).
checklist_ids:
  - UART-05
  - UART-06
  - UART-07
  - UART-08
test_names:
  - test_uart0_fifo_txrx
  - test_uart0_framing_error
  - test_uart0_baud_rates
  - test_uart_reg_smoke
priority: P1
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: uart_model
    type: uart
    connect:
      rx_from_soc: uart0_tx
      tx_to_soc: uart0_rx
    params:
      baud_rate: 115200
      data_bits: 8
      stop_bits: 1
      parity: none
      inject_framing_error: false

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset
  - step: 2
    action: apb_configure_uart
    instance: UART0
    baud_rate: 115200
    description: Configure UART0 at 115200 baud
  - step: 3
    action: apb_burst_write_uart_tx
    instance: UART0
    data: [0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, 0x66, 0x77]
    description: Write 8 bytes to UART0 TX FIFO (fills 8-deep FIFO)
  - step: 4
    action: wait_uart_tx_empty_irq
    instance: UART0
    timeout_cycles: 10000
    description: Wait for TX-empty interrupt after FIFO drains
  - step: 5
    action: check_irq
    irq_vector: 3
    description: Confirm UART0 TX-empty IRQ fires
  - step: 6
    action: configure_uart_model
    device: uart_model
    inject_framing_error: true
    description: Set uart_model to inject an invalid stop bit
  - step: 7
    action: uart_model_send_bad_frame
    device: uart_model
    data: 0xCC
    description: Send byte with bad stop bit from model to UART0 RX
  - step: 8
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: UART0
    register: STATUS
    mask: FERR_BIT
    expected: 1
    description: Verify FERR (framing error) flag is set in UART0 STATUS
  - step: 9
    action: apb_configure_uart
    instance: UART0
    baud_rate: 9600
    description: Reconfigure UART0 to 9600 baud
  - step: 10
    action: uart_loopback_byte
    instance: UART0
    baud_rate: 9600
    data: 0xF0
    description: Single-byte loopback at 9600 baud to verify divisor setting
  - step: 11
    action: apb_configure_uart
    instance: UART0
    baud_rate: 921600
    description: Reconfigure UART0 to 921600 baud
  - step: 12
    action: uart_loopback_byte
    instance: UART0
    baud_rate: 921600
    data: 0x0F
    description: Single-byte loopback at 921600 baud
  - step: 13
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [UART0, UART1, UART2, UART3]
    description: Read all registers across all four UART instances; verify reset defaults

checks:
  - id: CHK-UART-05
    checklist_id: UART-05
    description: TX-empty IRQ fires after 8-byte FIFO drains completely
    expected: UART0 TX-empty IRQ asserted; TXFIFO_EMPTY flag set
  - id: CHK-UART-06
    checklist_id: UART-06
    description: FERR flag set in UART0 STATUS after injected framing error
    expected: STATUS.FERR == 1; UART0 IRQ fires for error condition
  - id: CHK-UART-07
    checklist_id: UART-07
    description: UART0 operates correctly at 9600, 115200, and 921600 baud
    expected: Loopback byte received correctly at each baud rate
  - id: CHK-UART-08
    checklist_id: UART-08
    description: All UART CSRs across all four instances return expected reset values
    expected: No unexpected register values; no APB errors

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-UART-05 through CHK-UART-08 checks PASS
  - FERR flag clears after software acknowledgment
  - No simulation hang during FIFO drain wait
"}

{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_spi_transfer.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_spi_transfer
description: >
  Verifies SPI0 and SPI1 master-mode operation on mspm33c321a_derivative.
  Tests cover SPI mode 0 (CPOL=0,CPHA=0) and mode 3 (CPOL=1,CPHA=1)
  full-duplex transfers, 16-bit frame mode on SPI0, SPI1 baseline transfer,
  and register smoke test across both instances.
checklist_ids:
  - SPI-01
  - SPI-02
  - SPI-03
  - SPI-04
  - SPI-05
test_names:
  - test_spi0_mode0_transfer
  - test_spi0_mode3_transfer
  - test_spi1_transfer
  - test_spi0_16bit_frame
  - test_spi_reg_smoke
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: spi_slave_model
    type: spi_slave
    connect:
      sclk: spi0_sclk
      mosi: spi0_mosi
      miso: spi0_miso
      nss:  spi0_nss
    params:
      cpol: 0
      cpha: 0
      frame_bits: 8
      loopback: true
  - name: spi1_slave_model
    type: spi_slave
    connect:
      sclk: spi1_sclk
      mosi: spi1_mosi
      miso: spi1_miso
      nss:  spi1_nss
    params:
      cpol: 0
      cpha: 0
      frame_bits: 8
      loopback: true

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset
  - step: 2
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI0
    mode: 0
    frame_bits: 8
    clock_div: 4
    description: Configure SPI0 for mode 0, 8-bit frame, clk/4 prescaler
  - step: 3
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0xA5
    description: Initiate SPI0 8-bit transfer; slave model echoes data back
  - step: 4
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI0
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0xA5
    description: Read SPI0 RX FIFO; verify echoed data matches transmitted
  - step: 5
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI0
    mode: 3
    frame_bits: 8
    description: Reconfigure SPI0 for mode 3 (CPOL=1, CPHA=1)
  - step: 6
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0x3C
    description: Mode-3 transfer; slave model echoes
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI0
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0x3C
    description: Verify mode-3 receive data
  - step: 8
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI1
    mode: 0
    frame_bits: 8
    description: Configure SPI1 for mode 0, 8-bit frame
  - step: 9
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI1
    tx_data: 0x7E
    description: SPI1 transfer with spi1_slave_model in loopback
  - step: 10
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI1
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0x7E
    description: Verify SPI1 receive data
  - step: 11
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI0
    mode: 0
    frame_bits: 16
    description: Reconfigure SPI0 for 16-bit frame mode
  - step: 12
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0xDEAD
    description: 16-bit frame transfer on SPI0
  - step: 13
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI0
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0xDEAD
    description: Verify 16-bit received word
  - step: 14
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [SPI0, SPI1]
    description: Read all SPI CSRs; verify reset-default values

checks:
  - id: CHK-SPI-01
    checklist_id: SPI-01
    description: SPI0 mode-0 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0xA5
  - id: CHK-SPI-02
    checklist_id: SPI-02
    description: SPI0 mode-3 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0x3C
  - id: CHK-SPI-03
    checklist_id: SPI-03
    description: SPI1 mode-0 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0x7E
  - id: CHK-SPI-04
    checklist_id: SPI-04
    description: SPI0 16-bit frame transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0xDEAD
  - id: CHK-SPI-05
    checklist_id: SPI-05
    description: All SPI CSRs at both instances read expected reset defaults
    expected: No unexpected register values

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-SPI checks PASS
  - SPI slave models report no protocol errors
  - No APB errors during configuration or data access
"}

  full-duplex transfers, 16-bit frame mode on SPI0, SPI1 baseline transfer,
  and register smoke test across both instances.
checklist_ids:
  - SPI-01
  - SPI-02
  - SPI-03
  - SPI-04
  - SPI-05
test_names:
  - test_spi0_mode0_transfer
  - test_spi0_mode3_transfer
  - test_spi1_transfer
  - test_spi0_16bit_frame
  - test_spi_reg_smoke
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: spi_slave_model
    type: spi_slave
    connect:
      sclk: spi0_sclk
      mosi: spi0_mosi
      miso: spi0_miso
      nss:  spi0_nss
    params:
      cpol: 0
      cpha: 0
      frame_bits: 8
      loopback: true
  - name: spi1_slave_model
    type: spi_slave
    connect:
      sclk: spi1_sclk
      mosi: spi1_mosi
      miso: spi1_miso

    params:
      cpol: 0
      cpha: 0
      frame_bits: 8
      loopback: true

    description: Release reset
  - step: 2
    action: apb_configure_spi

    clock_div: 4
    description: Configure SPI0 for mode 0, 8-bit frame, clk/4 prescaler

    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0xA5

    register: RXDATA

    mode: 3

    description: Reconfigure SPI0 for mode 3 (CPOL=1, CPHA=1)
  - step: 6
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0x3C
    description: Mode-3 transfer; slave model echoes
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI0
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0x3C
    description: Verify mode-3 receive data
  - step: 8
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI1

    frame_bits: 8

  - step: 9
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI1
    tx_data: 0x7E
    description: SPI1 transfer with spi1_slave_model in loopback
  - step: 10
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI1
    register: RXDATA
    expected: 0x7E
    description: Verify SPI1 receive data
  - step: 11
    action: apb_configure_spi
    instance: SPI0
    mode: 0
    frame_bits: 16
    description: Reconfigure SPI0 for 16-bit frame mode
  - step: 12
    action: spi_master_transfer
    instance: SPI0
    tx_data: 0xDEAD
    description: 16-bit frame transfer on SPI0
  - step: 13
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: SPI0

    expected: 0xDEAD
    description: Verify 16-bit received word
  - step: 14
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [SPI0, SPI1]
    description: Read all SPI CSRs; verify reset-default values

checks:
  - id: CHK-SPI-01
    checklist_id: SPI-01
    description: SPI0 mode-0 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0xA5
  - id: CHK-SPI-02
    checklist_id: SPI-02
    description: SPI0 mode-3 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0x3C
  - id: CHK-SPI-03
    checklist_id: SPI-03
    description: SPI1 mode-0 8-bit transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0x7E
  - id: CHK-SPI-04
    checklist_id: SPI-04
    description: SPI0 16-bit frame transfer echoed correctly
    expected: RXDATA == 0xDEAD
  - id: CHK-SPI-05
    checklist_id: SPI-05
    description: All SPI CSRs at both instances read expected reset defaults
    expected: No unexpected register values

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-SPI checks PASS
  - SPI slave models report no protocol errors
  - No APB errors during configuration or data access

scenario_name: scenario_i2c_transfer
description: >
  Verifies all three I2C instances (I2C0, I2C1, I2C2) of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  I2C2 is the derivative-added instance (R-006). Tests cover 7-bit address
  write/read transactions on all instances, 10-bit address mode on I2C0,
  fast-mode (400 kHz) and fast-plus-mode (1 MHz) on I2C0, and register
  smoke test across all three instances. Each instance uses an independent
  I2C slave memory model.
checklist_ids:
  - I2C-01
  - I2C-02
  - I2C-03
  - I2C-04
  - I2C-05
  - I2C-06
  - I2C-07
  - I2C-08
test_names:
  - test_i2c0_7bit_write
  - test_i2c0_7bit_read
  - test_i2c1_transfer
  - test_i2c2_transfer
  - test_i2c0_10bit_addr
  - test_i2c0_fast_mode
  - test_i2c0_fast_plus
  - test_i2c_reg_smoke
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:

    type: i2c_memory
    connect:
      sda: i2c0_sda
      scl: i2c0_scl
    params:
      address_7bit: 0x50
      mem_size_bytes: 256
      speed_hz: 100000
  - name: i2c1_slave_model
    type: i2c_memory
    connect:
      sda: i2c1_sda
      scl: i2c1_scl
    params:
      address_7bit: 0x51
      mem_size_bytes: 256
      speed_hz: 100000
  - name: i2c2_slave_model
    type: i2c_memory
    connect:
      sda: i2c2_sda
      scl: i2c2_scl
    params:
      address_7bit: 0x52
      mem_size_bytes: 256
      speed_hz: 100000

    action: apb_configure_i2c
    instance: I2C1
    mode: standard_100kHz
    addr_mode: 7bit
    description: Configure I2C1 at 100 kHz, 7-bit addressing
  - step: 6
    action: i2c_master_write_read
    instance: I2C1
    slave_addr: 0x51
    reg_addr: 0x10
    write_data: 0xCD
    description: I2C1 write and read-back test
  - step: 7
    action: apb_configure_i2c
    instance: I2C2
    mode: standard_100kHz
    addr_mode: 7bit
    description: Configure derivative I2C2 instance at 100 kHz
  - step: 8
    action: i2c_master_write_read
    instance: I2C2
    slave_addr: 0x52
    reg_addr: 0x20
    write_data: 0xEF
    description: I2C2 (new derivative instance) write and read-back test

  - step: 10

  - step: 11

    instance: I2C0
    mode: fast_400kHz
    addr_mode: 7bit
    description: Switch I2C0 to fast-mode 400 kHz
  - step: 12
    action: i2c_master_write_read
    instance: I2C0

    reg_addr: 0x05
    write_data: 0x11
    description: Fast-mode transaction at 400 kHz
  - step: 13
    action: apb_configure_i2c
    instance: I2C0
    mode: fast_plus_1MHz
    addr_mode: 7bit
    description: Switch I2C0 to fast-plus mode 1 MHz
  - step: 14
    action: i2c_master_write_read
    instance: I2C0
    slave_addr: 0x50
    reg_addr: 0x06
    write_data: 0x22
    description: Fast-plus-mode transaction at 1 MHz
  - step: 15
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [I2C0, I2C1, I2C2]
    description: Read all I2C CSRs; verify reset-value defaults for all three instances

checks:
  - id: CHK-I2C-01
    checklist_id: I2C-01
    description: I2C0 7-bit write 0xAB to slave ACKed; slave registers value
    expected: I2C0 STATUS.BUSY clears; no NACK; slave contains 0xAB at reg 0x00
  - id: CHK-I2C-02
    checklist_id: I2C-02
    description: I2C0 7-bit read from slave returns 0xAB
    expected: RXDATA == 0xAB
  - id: CHK-I2C-03
    checklist_id: I2C-03
    description: I2C1 write/read cycle completes correctly
    expected: Written value read back from I2C1 slave
  - id: CHK-I2C-04
    checklist_id: I2C-04
    description: I2C2 (derivative new instance) write/read cycle completes correctly
    expected: Written value read back from I2C2 slave; NACK not observed
  - id: CHK-I2C-05
    checklist_id: I2C-05
    description: I2C0 10-bit address transaction ACKed; data verified
    expected: 10-bit slave at 0x150 responds; data matches
  - id: CHK-I2C-06
    checklist_id: I2C-06
    description: I2C0 400 kHz fast-mode transaction completes without errors
    expected: Transfer completes; no timeout; slave ACKs
  - id: CHK-I2C-07
    checklist_id: I2C-07
    description: I2C0 1 MHz fast-plus-mode transaction completes without errors
    expected: Transfer completes; no timeout; slave ACKs
  - id: CHK-I2C-08
    checklist_id: I2C-08
    description: All I2C CSRs across I2C0, I2C1, I2C2 read expected reset defaults
    expected: No unexpected values; no APB errors

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-I2C checks PASS
  - I2C slave models report no protocol violations
  - I2C2 (derivative instance) behaves identically to I2C0/I2C1

{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_timer_basic.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_timer_basic
description: >
  Verifies all six timer instances (TMR0–TMR5) of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  TMR0–TMR3 are general-purpose 16-bit timers; TMR4–TMR5 are advanced control
  timers. Tests cover overflow interrupt, input capture, compare-match
  interrupt, prescaler configuration, up/down count mode (TMR4), and
  center-aligned count mode (TMR5), plus register smoke test for all instances.
checklist_ids:
  - TMR-01
  - TMR-02
  - TMR-03
  - TMR-04
  - TMR-05
  - TMR-06
  - TMR-07
test_names:
  - test_tmr0_overflow_irq
  - test_tmr1_input_capture
  - test_tmr2_compare_irq
  - test_tmr3_prescaler
  - test_tmr4_updown_count
  - test_tmr5_center_aligned
  - test_tmr_reg_smoke
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: tmr1_capture_in
    params:
      pulse_width_ns: 100
      assert_polarity: high

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset
  - step: 2
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR0
    mode: up_count
    period: 0xFFFF
    prescaler: 1
    irq_enable: overflow
    description: Configure TMR0 as up-count timer; enable overflow IRQ
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: TMR0
    register: CR1
    value: CEN_BIT
    description: Enable TMR0 counter
  - step: 4
    action: wait_irq
    irq_vector: 12
    timeout_cycles: 200000
    description: Wait for TMR0 overflow IRQ (IRQ12) to fire
  - step: 5
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR1
    mode: input_capture
    capture_edge: rising
    description: Configure TMR1 in input capture mode on rising edge
  - step: 6
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    description: Pulse TMR1 capture input via gpio_stimulus model
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: TMR1
    register: CCR1
    description: Read TMR1 capture register; verify captured value is non-zero
  - step: 8
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR2
    mode: up_count
    period: 0x1000
    compare_value: 0x0800
    irq_enable: compare_match
    description: Configure TMR2 with compare match at half period
  - step: 9
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: TMR2
    register: CR1
    value: CEN_BIT
    description: Enable TMR2
  - step: 10
    action: wait_irq
    irq_vector: 14
    timeout_cycles: 50000
    description: Wait for TMR2 compare-match IRQ (IRQ14)
  - step: 11
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR3
    mode: up_count
    period: 0x00FF
    prescaler_values: [1, 4, 16, 64]
    description: Test TMR3 with multiple prescaler values; measure resulting period
  - step: 12
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR4
    mode: up_down_count
    period: 0x07FF
    description: Configure TMR4 advanced timer in up/down count mode
  - step: 13
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: TMR4
    register: CR1
    value: CEN_BIT
    description: Enable TMR4 and observe counting direction changes
  - step: 14
    action: apb_configure_timer
    instance: TMR5
    mode: center_aligned
    period: 0x03FF
    description: Configure TMR5 advanced timer in center-aligned (edge-aligned) mode
  - step: 15
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: TMR5
    register: CR1
    value: CEN_BIT
    description: Enable TMR5 center-aligned counter
  - step: 16
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [TMR0, TMR1, TMR2, TMR3, TMR4, TMR5]
    description: Read all timer CSRs across all six instances

checks:
  - id: CHK-TMR-01
    checklist_id: TMR-01
    description: TMR0 overflow IRQ fires within expected period; ISR acknowledges
    expected: IRQ12 asserts after 0xFFFF count cycles; ISR clears flag
  - id: CHK-TMR-02
    checklist_id: TMR-02
    description: TMR1 CCR1 latches non-zero value after capture input pulse
    expected: CCR1 != 0x0000 after stimulus pulse
  - id: CHK-TMR-03
    checklist_id: TMR-03
    description: TMR2 compare-match IRQ fires when count reaches 0x0800
    expected: IRQ14 asserts at count == 0x0800
  - id: CHK-TMR-04
    checklist_id: TMR-04
    description: TMR3 period scales correctly with prescaler values 1/4/16/64
    expected: Measured period ratio matches prescaler ratio
  - id: CHK-TMR-05
    checklist_id: TMR-05
    description: TMR4 count direction reverses at period and zero boundaries
    expected: Count increments to 0x07FF then decrements back to 0
  - id: CHK-TMR-06
    checklist_id: TMR-06
    description: TMR5 center-aligned counter operates symmetrically
    expected: Count peaks at 0x03FF and reverses; overflow IRQ at center
  - id: CHK-TMR-07
    checklist_id: TMR-07
    description: All timer CSRs across all six instances at reset defaults
    expected: No unexpected register values

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-TMR checks PASS
  - No simulation timeout waiting for timer IRQs
  - No APB errors during timer configuration
"}

  center-aligned count mode (TMR5), plus register smoke test for all instances.

  - TMR-01
  - TMR-02
  - TMR-03
  - TMR-04
  - TMR-05
  - TMR-06
  - TMR-07

  - test_tmr2_compare_irq

  - test_tmr5_center_aligned
  - test_tmr_reg_smoke

  - step: 9
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: TMR2
    register: CR1
    value: CEN_BIT
    description: Enable TMR2
  - step: 10
    action: wait_irq
    irq_vector: 14
    timeout_cycles: 50000
    description: Wait for TMR2 compare-match IRQ (IRQ14)
  - step: 11

function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_pwm_output.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_pwm_output
description: >
  Verifies PWM0 and PWM1 instances of mspm33c321a_derivative. Tests cover
  period and duty-cycle measurement on PWM0, complementary output with
  dead-time insertion on PWM1 (no shoot-through), fault input forcing safe
  state on PWM0, and register smoke test on both instances.
checklist_ids:
  - PWM-01
  - PWM-02
  - PWM-03
  - PWM-04
test_names:
  - test_pwm0_period_duty
  - test_pwm1_complementary_deadtime
  - test_pwm0_fault_response
  - test_pwm_reg_smoke
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: pwm0_fault_in
    params:
      pulse_width_ns: 250
      assert_polarity: high

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset
  - step: 2
    action: apb_configure_pwm
    instance: PWM0
    period_counts: 0x0FFF
    duty_counts: 0x07FF
    mode: edge_aligned
    description: Configure PWM0 period=0x0FFF, duty=50% (0x07FF)
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: PWM0
    register: CR1
    value: MOE_BIT | CEN_BIT
    description: Enable PWM0 main output and counter
  - step: 4
    action: measure_pwm_output
    signal: pwm0_out
    expected_period_counts: 0x0FFF
    expected_high_counts: 0x07FF
    tolerance_percent: 1.0
    description: Measure PWM0 output period and high-time
  - step: 5
    action: apb_configure_pwm
    instance: PWM1
    period_counts: 0x07FF
    duty_counts: 0x03FF
    mode: complementary
    dead_time_counts: 8
    description: Configure PWM1 complementary pair with 8-cycle dead time
  - step: 6
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: PWM1
    register: CR1
    value: MOE_BIT | CEN_BIT
    description: Enable PWM1
  - step: 7
    action: verify_no_shootthrough
    signal_p: pwm1_out_p
    signal_n: pwm1_out_n
    min_deadtime_counts: 8
    description: Verify PWM1 complementary outputs never overlap
  - step: 8
    action: apb_configure_pwm
    instance: PWM0
    fault_input_enable: true
    description: Enable fault input on PWM0
  - step: 9
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    description: Assert PWM0 fault input via gpio_stimulus model
  - step: 10
    action: verify_pwm_safe_state
    signal: pwm0_out
    expected_state: low
    description: Verify PWM0 output forced to safe (low) state on fault assertion
  - step: 11
    action: apb_read_all_csr
    peripherals: [PWM0, PWM1]
    description: Read all PWM CSRs; verify reset defaults

checks:
  - id: CHK-PWM-01
    checklist_id: PWM-01
    description: PWM0 output period and high-time within 1% of configured values
    expected: period = 0x0FFF cycles ± 1%; duty = 50% ± 1%
  - id: CHK-PWM-02
    checklist_id: PWM-02
    description: PWM1 complementary outputs never simultaneously high; dead-time ≥ 8 cycles
    expected: min dead time observed ≥ 8 clk cycles; no shoot-through
  - id: CHK-PWM-03
    checklist_id: PWM-03
    description: PWM0 fault input forces output to safe (low) state immediately
    expected: pwm0_out goes low within 1 cycle of fault assertion
  - id: CHK-PWM-04
    checklist_id: PWM-04
    description: All PWM CSRs at reset defaults for both instances
    expected: No unexpected register values

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-PWM checks PASS
  - No shoot-through condition observed on PWM1
  - Fault response within 1 clock cycle
"}

external_devices:

dma_params:

stimulus:

  - step: 2
    action: fill_sram

    size: 256
    pattern: INCREMENTING
    description: Fill source buffer with 0x00..0xFF incrementing pattern
  - step: 3
    action: apb_configure_dma_channel
    channel: 0
    mode: mem_to_mem
    src_addr: 0x20000000
    dst_addr: 0x20010000
    length: 256
    irq_enable: transfer_complete
    description: Configure DMA channel 0 for 256-byte M2M transfer

    action: apb_write
    peripheral: DMAC
    register: CH0_CR
    value: EN_BIT
    description: Enable DMA channel 0

    action: wait_irq

    timeout_cycles: 50000
    description: Wait for DMA transfer-complete IRQ (IRQ20)

    action: compare_memory
    src_addr: 0x20000000
    dst_addr: 0x20010000
    size: 256

    action: uart_model_send_block
    device: uart_model
    data_block: [0x10, 0x20, 0x30, 0x40, 0x50, 0x60, 0x70, 0x80]

  - step: 8
    action: apb_configure_dma_channel
    channel: 1
    mode: periph_to_mem
    src_periph: UART0_RX
    dst_addr: 0x20020000
    length: 8
    irq_enable: transfer_complete
    description: Configure DMA channel 1 to receive UART0 RX data into SRAM
  - step: 9
    action: apb_configure_dma_channel
    channel: 2
    mode: mem_to_periph
    src_addr: 0x20030000
    dst_periph: UART0_TX
    length: 8
    irq_enable: transfer_complete
    description: Configure DMA channel 2 to transmit SRAM data via UART0 TX
  - step: 10
    action: configure_all_8_channels
    base_src: 0x20000000
    base_dst: 0x20008000
    length_each: 32
    description: Configure all 8 DMA channels for simultaneous M2M transfers
  - step: 11
    action: enable_all_channels
    description: Simultaneously enable all 8 DMA channels
  - step: 12
    action: wait_all_done
    timeout_cycles: 500000
    description: Wait for all 8 channel transfer-complete flags
  - step: 13
    action: apb_configure_dma_channel
    channel: 3
    mode: invalid_config
    description: Configure DMA channel 3 with invalid src/dst alignment to trigger error
  - step: 14
    action: wait_irq
    irq_vector: 20
    irq_type: error
    timeout_cycles: 10000
    description: Wait for DMA error IRQ
  - step: 15
    action: set_channel_priority
    channel_high: 7
    channel_low: 0
    description: Set channel 7 to highest priority and channel 0 to lowest
  - step: 16
    action: trigger_simultaneous_request
    channels: [0, 7]
    description: Simultaneously trigger channels 0 and 7; verify channel 7 is served first

checks:
  - id: CHK-DMA-01
    checklist_id: DMA-01
    description: 256-byte M2M transfer destination matches source exactly
    expected: Zero mismatches in buffer comparison
  - id: CHK-DMA-02
    checklist_id: DMA-02
    description: UART0 RX bytes arrive in SRAM destination buffer via DMA
    expected: SRAM at 0x20020000 contains [0x10,0x20,...,0x80]
  - id: CHK-DMA-03
    checklist_id: DMA-03
    description: SRAM data transmitted via UART0 TX through DMA; model receives correct bytes
    expected: uart_model receives all bytes in order
  - id: CHK-DMA-04
    checklist_id: DMA-04
    description: All 8 concurrent DMA transfers complete without data corruption
    expected: All 8 destination buffers match source buffers
  - id: CHK-DMA-05
    checklist_id: DMA-05
    description: Transfer-complete IRQ fires after each channel completes transfer
    expected: IRQ20 asserts; DONE flag set in DMAC status
  - id: CHK-DMA-06
    checklist_id: DMA-06
    description: DMA error IRQ fires on invalid transfer configuration
    expected: Error flag set; IRQ20 error variant asserts
  - id: CHK-DMA-07
    checklist_id: DMA-07
    description: Higher-priority channel 7 is serviced before lower-priority channel 0
    expected: Channel 7 transfer starts before channel 0 when simultaneously requested

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-DMA checks PASS
  - No data corruption across any concurrent transfer
  - uart_model reports clean protocol during DMA-UART tests
"}

  - step: 11

    description: Start AES decryption
  - step: 12

  - step: 14

    iv: [0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06, 0x07,

    description: Configure AES-128 CBC mode with standard IV
  - step: 16
    action: aes_dma_bulk_encrypt
    src_addr: 0x20000000
    dst_addr: 0x20001000
    length_bytes: 256
    dma_channel: 5
    description: Load 256-byte plaintext at 0x20000000; DMA through AES to 0x20001000

checks:
  - id: CHK-AES-01
    checklist_id: AES-01
    description: AES-128 ECB encryption output matches FIPS-197 expected ciphertext
    expected: Output == [0x3A,0xD7,0x7B,0xB4,...]
  - id: CHK-AES-02
    checklist_id: AES-02
    description: AES-128 ECB decryption of ciphertext recovers original plaintext

  - id: CHK-AES-05

  - id: CHK-AES-06
    checklist_id: AES-06
    description: AES done IRQ (IRQ22) fires within 100 cycles of operation start
    expected: IRQ asserts; DONE flag set in AES STATUS register

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-AES checks PASS
  - All KAT vectors match bit-exactly
  - No AES engine hang or timeout
